files.summary: Application entry point that wires all layers together and prints a greeting to stdout.
files.description: HelloWorld is the bootstrapping class for the LegacyFinApp-026 application. It manually instantiates the three-layer architecture (repository, service, controller) using constructor-based dependency injection. It delegates greeting generation to the controller and outputs the result to standard output. This class owns the object composition graph and the application lifecycle start-up sequence.
business_rules: When no command-line arguments are provided the application must still produce a valid greeting using the default recipient
The output greeting must be written to standard output (stdout)
The application composes all layers at startup before invoking any business logic

method_breakdown.summary: Application entry point: composes the dependency graph, invokes the controller, and prints the greeting.
parameters: args (String[]): Optional command-line arguments; the first element, if present, is used as the greeting recipient
method_breakdown.significance: Sole entry point; owns the entire composition root and triggers the full greeting workflow

files.summary: Controller layer that parses raw input arguments and delegates greeting creation to the service layer.
files.description: GreetingController acts as the input-processing facade between the CLI entry point and the domain service. It validates whether command-line arguments were provided and extracts the first argument as the intended recipient. It enforces a non-null contract on its GreetingService dependency at construction time. When no arguments are available it passes null to the service, triggering the default-recipient fallback logic. This class isolates the input-parsing concern from business logic.
business_rules: The controller requires a non-null GreetingService dependency; construction fails with an exception if null is supplied
When args is null or empty, the controller passes null as the recipient to trigger the default-recipient fallback
Only the first command-line argument (args[0]) is used as the recipient name; additional arguments are ignored

method_breakdown.summary: Constructs the controller with a mandatory GreetingService dependency; throws NullPointerException if the service is null.
parameters: greetingService (GreetingService): The service used to generate greetings; must not be null
method_breakdown.significance: Enforces non-null dependency injection contract at object creation time

method_breakdown.summary: Parses CLI args, extracts the first argument as the recipient, and delegates to GreetingService.createGreeting.
parameters: args (String[]): Raw command-line arguments array; may be null or empty
method_breakdown.significance: Primary business method; bridges raw input to the domain greeting logic

files.summary: Domain service that constructs a personalised greeting by resolving the recipient and applying the greeting template.
files.description: GreetingService contains the core business logic for greeting creation in LegacyFinApp-026. It determines the appropriate recipient by falling back to a repository-supplied default when the requested recipient is null or blank. Recipient whitespace is trimmed before use to ensure clean output. The service composes the final greeting string by applying the repository-provided template with the resolved recipient. It enforces a non-null repository dependency at construction time.
business_rules: If the requested recipient is null the service must fall back to the repository default recipient
If the requested recipient is blank (whitespace-only) the service must fall back to the repository default recipient
A non-blank requested recipient must have leading and trailing whitespace trimmed before use
The greeting is formed by substituting the resolved recipient into the repository greeting template
A non-null GreetingRepository must be provided at construction time; null injection is rejected

method_breakdown.summary: Constructs the service with a mandatory GreetingRepository; throws NullPointerException if repository is null.
parameters: greetingRepository (GreetingRepository): The repository supplying templates and defaults; must not be null
method_breakdown.significance: Enforces non-null dependency at construction; prevents runtime NPEs downstream

method_breakdown.summary: Resolves the effective recipient (using default if input is null/blank, trimming otherwise) and formats the greeting template.
parameters: requestedRecipient (String): The caller-supplied recipient name; may be null or blank
method_breakdown.returns: String: The formatted greeting string, e.g. 'Hello World'
method_breakdown.significance: Core business logic method; implements the recipient-resolution rules and message composition

files.summary: Data-access layer that supplies static greeting configuration: the message template and the default recipient.
files.description: GreetingRepository serves as the data-source abstraction for greeting configuration in LegacyFinApp-026. It provides two pieces of static domain data: the greeting message template and the default recipient name. In this version both values are hard-coded constants, making the repository a thin configuration provider. The class represents the bottom of the three-tier architecture and has no external dependencies. It establishes the canonical greeting format used throughout the application.
business_rules: The greeting template is 'Hello %s' where %s is the recipient placeholder
The greeting template must contain exactly one positional string placeholder for the recipient

method_breakdown.summary: Returns the greeting message format string containing a single recipient placeholder.
method_breakdown.significance: Defines the universal greeting format; changing this method changes all greeting output

method_breakdown.summary: Returns the fallback recipient name used when no recipient is specified.
method_breakdown.significance: Determines the default output when the application is run without arguments

files.summary: JUnit 5 test suite verifying the end-to-end greeting output and individual layer behaviours.
files.description: HelloWorldTest validates the core business rules of LegacyFinApp-026 using three focused test cases. It covers the full integration path from main() through to stdout, the controller's argument-routing behaviour, and the service's blank-input fallback logic. The test suite captures stdout by redirecting System.out to a ByteArrayOutputStream to assert exact output including newline. It uses JUnit Jupiter assertions exclusively.
business_rules: The application must print 'Hello World' followed by a system newline when invoked with no arguments
The controller must use the first argument as the greeting recipient producing 'Hello Copilot' for input 'Copilot'
The service must produce 'Hello World' when given a blank (whitespace-only) recipient string

method_breakdown.summary: Integration test: invokes HelloWorld.main with empty args array and asserts stdout contains 'Hello World\n'.
method_breakdown.significance: End-to-end smoke test; validates the complete composition root and default-recipient path

method_breakdown.summary: Unit test: verifies the controller extracts args[0] as the recipient and returns 'Hello Copilot'.
method_breakdown.significance: Validates the named-recipient happy path through controller and service

method_breakdown.summary: Unit test: verifies that a blank string (spaces only) triggers the default-recipient fallback in the service.
method_breakdown.significance: Validates the critical blank-input edge case and the whitespace-fallback business rule

files.summary: Maven build descriptor defining project coordinates, Java 25 compiler settings, and JUnit 5 test dependency.
files.description: The pom.xml configures the LegacyFinApp-026 build lifecycle using Apache Maven. It sets the project group, artifact, and version and targets Java 25 source and bytecode compatibility. The sole runtime-scoped external dependency is JUnit Jupiter 5.11.4, scoped to test only. Maven Compiler Plugin 3.13.0 and Maven Surefire Plugin 3.5.2 are explicitly versioned to ensure reproducible builds. UTF-8 is enforced as the source file encoding.

design_patterns.description: All three application classes (HelloWorld, GreetingController, GreetingService) receive their dependencies through constructor parameters rather than field injection or service locators.
The application is structured into three explicit horizontal layers: Presentation/Controller, Business Logic/Service, and Data Access/Repository, each with a single responsibility.
The service provides a default recipient ('World') when no valid input is supplied, preventing null propagation through the system.
HelloWorld.main() acts as the single composition root where the entire object graph is assembled before any business logic executes.

application_summary.name: LegacyFinApp-026 (Hello World)
application_summary.purpose: A minimal Java console application that prints a personalised greeting to standard output.
key_functionalities: Accept an optional recipient name as a CLI argument
Fall back to 'World' as the default recipient when none is provided
Trim whitespace from user-supplied recipient names
Format and output a greeting using a configurable template
application_summary.architecture: Three-tier layered architecture with constructor-based dependency injection and a composition-root entry point
